arts.ttl: Goal and assist: “Great” Leeds United man lauded in chaotic Europa League clash
arts.sum: Leeds United right-back Rasmus Kristensen is making a strong impression at Bundesliga club Eintracht Frankfurt while on loan, getting off the mark with a goal in the Europa League on Thursday. Helping Eintracht to a two-goal lead in the second half was Kristensen, who set up the game’s opening goal as he slipped a brilliant pass through to talented striker Hugo Ekitike for 1-0.

arts.ttl: £5m Leeds United man facing one of biggest tests of the season tomorrow
arts.sum: Leeds United right-back Jayden Bogle has faced criticism for his start to life in West Yorkshire, but his performance against Cardiff City was comfortably his best so far. The right-back in turn produced his best display since joining Leeds from Sheffield United, creating chances aplenty with two key passes, winning eight duels and winning a penalty – obviously not converted by Pascal Struijk.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins turns up heat on Leeds United as he highlights big Coventry City difference
arts.sum: While Mark Robins knows the current focus at Coventry City is to start picking up more points, the manager insists the pressure is on his opposite number, Daniel Farke, ahead of this week’s match at Leeds United. But the pressure is different at Leeds United than it is at Coventry City, albeit we want the same end goal, and I think the demands are greater for Daniel than they are for me.”

arts.ttl: Beren Cross's Leeds United notebook on 'dire' Elland Road need, squad photo and new Thorp Arch tech
arts.sum: The big news of the week at Leeds United has been the statement on the future ambitions for Elland Road’s redevelopment. Leeds United Supporters’ Trust sent LeedsLive a statement with its reaction to the news.

arts.ttl: David Prutton predicts outcome of Leeds United v Coventry City, goes against recent record
arts.sum: Leeds United host Coventry City in West Yorkshire this weekend looking to make it four wins out of five games, but face a significant test against the Sky Blues. Coventry were one of only four Championship sides that were unbeaten against Leeds last season, taking four points from Farke’s men across two games, including a draw at Elland Road against the run of play.

arts.ttl: Coventry City's tactical tweak nine months on from Leeds United boss Daniel Farke's frustration
arts.sum: Leeds United boss Daniel Farke couldn’t hide his anger and frustration after Coventry City earned a hard-fought point at Elland Road last season – a result they followed up superbly by taking all three in a 2-1 victory at the CBS Arena in the return Championship fixture. Farke appeared particularly aggrieved at Mark Robins’ tactics, which saw centre-half Joel Latibeaudiere deployed in midfield and left-back Jay Dasilva pushed forward to the wing ahead of Jake Bidwell, leaving Haji Wright and Callum O’Hare on the Sky Blues bench.

arts.ttl: Sheffield United have been proven right over £13m Leeds United transfer snub
arts.sum: Blades boss Chris Wilder was left deeply unimpressed over the Whites' transfer pursuit of Gustavo Hamer. By joining the Leeds United community on WhatsApp, you'll see the biggest and best stories as soon as they happen.

arts.ttl: Marcelo Bielsa’s 515-word Eddie Nketiah monologue shows why Joel Piroe shouldn’t start for Leeds United
arts.sum: Leeds United forwards Joel Piroe and Mateo Joseph are creating a strong debate about who should start up front, with the former scoring against Cardiff City and the latter assisting the opener. The Argentine underlined the differences in Bamford and Nketiah, one excelling at everything other than scoring by helping create chances and running for the good of the team, while Nketiah’s sole benefit was in his goalscoring, struggling to offer much else, as Bielsa said (via the Mirror ):

arts.ttl: Mark Robins clarifies post-match comments aimed at ‘one or two’ Coventry City players
arts.sum: But I have said before, there are 23 other teams that want the same thing at this level and it’s really, really difficult, and doesn’t automatically happen. So it’s not a criticism at all, although in the moment you want a better performance and more aggressive performance than we gave in the first half, so that was the thing.

arts.ttl: Leeds United transfer point made as Coventry City boss hits out at 'nasty' social media critics
arts.sum: Mark Robins admits social media can be a "really nasty place" as his out-of-form Coventry City side prepare to meet Leeds United. When asked about social media at his pre-match press conference and if it’s a place to stay away from, Robins told reporters: “I don’t participate in it and I really do not care.
